headline: This Is How You Track Risk Of Your Micron Stock Investment
summary: Micron Technology (MU) reported its Q2 FY26 earnings yesterday, March 18, and the data was staggering. Non-GAAP EPS hit $12.20, a huge jump vs last quarter that crushed consensus estimates. How did this happen? The revenue skyrocketed 196% year-over-year while gross margins expanded massively to nearly 75%. And despite a very strong guidance, the stock dropped roughly 4.4% in after-hours trading. Why.

headline: Micron shares slip as hefty spending plans eclipse strong AI-fueled earnings
summary: Micron's shares dropped more than 4% before the bell on Thursday, as the chipmaker's plans for heftier capex spending ‌unnerved investors, taking the shine off another round of AI-fueled blockbuster ‌quarterly earnings.  Micron, whose shares have climbed more than 61% this year after surging over 240% in 2025, ​said it is boosting its 2026 capital spending plan by $5 billion to meet growing demand, bringing its total investment for the current fiscal year to more than $25 billion.  "Investors wager that these are peak earnings and will be unsustainable," said Mike O'Rourke, chief ​market ​strategist at JonesTrading.

headline: Micron Earnings Were Extraordinary. The Stock Is Dropping.
summary: Micron Technology  reported exceptional second-quarter earnings results.  Guidance for the third quarter is equally stunning, with a projection of $33.5 billion in sales and a whopping 81% gross margin, even better than  Nvidia’s  Adjusted EPS are expected to rise to $19.15, a 903% jump from the year before.  Far from slowing down, Micron is accelerating in the current quarter.

headline: Why Micron’s Forecast Is Too Good—Heard on the Street
summary: Which explains why Micron’s blowout quarterly results have investors asking if things are as good as they can get.  Micron’s fiscal second-quarter results posted late Wednesday were a blowout by any measure.  Revenue nearly tripled year over year to $23.9 billion, while adjusted operating earnings of $16.5 billion beat Wall Street’s consensus estimate by 33%.

headline: MU Q1 Deep Dive: AI Demand, Tight Supply, and Strategic Agreements Shape Outlook
summary: Memory chips maker Micron (NYSE:MU) reported revenue ahead of Wall Street’s expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 196% year on year to $23.86 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($33.5 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 41.4% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $12.20 per share was 38.7% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: MU Q1 Deep Dive: AI Demand, Tight Supply, and Strategic Agreements Shape Outlook
content.summary: Memory chips maker Micron (NYSE:MU) reported revenue ahead of Wall Street’s expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 196% year on year to $23.86 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($33.5 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 41.4% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $12.20 per share was 38.7% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Apple’s China iPhone Sales Are Up. Thank Memory Cost Increases.
summary: Apple  iPhone sales outperformed competitors in China, new data show, signaling the company’s ability to persevere against a volatile industry backdrop.  For the first nine weeks of 2026, overall China smartphone sales declined 4% from the prior year, according to a report published by Counterpoint Research on Thursday.  This has caused a shortage in supply, but it’s been a tailwind for suppliers such as  Micron Technology  which reported second-quarter revenue growth of 196% on Wednesday night.

content.title: Apple’s China iPhone Sales Are Up. Thank Memory Cost Increases.
content.summary: Apple  iPhone sales outperformed competitors in China, new data show, signaling the company’s ability to persevere against a volatile industry backdrop.  For the first nine weeks of 2026, overall China smartphone sales declined 4% from the prior year, according to a report published by Counterpoint Research on Thursday.  This has caused a shortage in supply, but it’s been a tailwind for suppliers such as  Micron Technology  which reported second-quarter revenue growth of 196% on Wednesday night.

headline: Micron Slips 4%: NVIDIA’s HBM Supplier Just Posted Record Results, So Why Is It Falling?
summary: Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) is down 4% in Thursday trading, with shares sliding to $445 from a prior close of $461.73. That’s a head-scratcher on the surface, because last night Micron posted what CEO Sanjay Mehrotra called record results across the board. headline: Why Micron stock is falling despite Q2 earnings beat
summary: Micron (MU) stock is moving lower even after the company beat second quarter earnings expectations. Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland chats with Morning Brief host Julie Hyman to discuss the market's reaction and the key drivers behind the stock moves.

content.title: Why Micron stock is falling despite Q2 earnings beat
content.description:
Micron (MU) stock is moving lower even after the company beat second quarter earnings expectations.

Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland chats with Morning Brief host Julie Hyman to discuss the market's reaction and the key drivers behind the stock moves.
content.summary: Micron (MU) stock is moving lower even after the company beat second quarter earnings expectations. Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland chats with Morning Brief host Julie Hyman to discuss the market's reaction and the key drivers behind the stock moves.
